commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: "Now may Israel say. " She now seemeth to be speaking of herself: for she seemed not to have commenced herself, but to have answered. But to whom hath she replied. To them that think and say, How great evils do we endure, how great are the scandals that every day thicken, as the wicked enter into the Church, and we have to endure them. But let the Church reply through some, that is, through the voice of the stronger, let her reply to the complaints of the weak, and let the stable confirm the unstable, and the full-grown the infant, and let the Church say, "Many a time have they vexed me from my youth up" (ver. Let the Church say this: let her not fear it. For what is the meaning of this addition, "From my youth up," after the words, "Many a time have they fought against me". At present the old age of the Church is assailed: but let her not fear. Hath she then failed to arrive at old age, because they have not ceased to fight against her from her youth up. have they been able to blot her out. Let Israel comfort herself, let the Church console herself with past examples. Why have they fought against me. "For they could not prevail against me.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
But. Or "for "etenim. On this account they repeated their attacks. (Haydock)
But the psalmist testifies that they will not succeed. (Worthington)
